mechanics.breadcrumb_path: Rules Compendium > Page 181 > locations > All citizens are entitled to free pri- mary
mechanics.excerpt: All citizens are entitled to free pri- mary and secondary schooling; where necessary, the govern- ment often bears the expense of transporting children to worlds where these services are available and pays for their room and board_ The schools are often low quality; however; and the cur- riculum includes an inordinate amount of political indoctrina tion: The Confederation has very few higher-level schools_ small number of colleges and universities are the province nobility, warriors and those few from the lower castes who can prove their academic worth.
